messages.content:
A company is considering several Customer Relationship Management (CRM) providers and wants to understand what customizations each platform can offer. What is a good indicator of a CRM software’s customization capability?


-the amount of user traffic the CRM platform can handle


-the market share held by the CRM platform provider


-the quality of the user interface and user experience


-the robustness of the Application Programming Interface (API)


-I don't know this yet.

The robustness of the Application Programming Interface (API) is a good indicator of a CRM software's customization capability. A strong API indicates that the CRM platform can be integrated with other systems and customized to meet specific business needs.

An Accenture client is currently using Salesforce as its Customer Relationship Management (CRM) solution. The client would like to implement an AI application to help predict future customer behavior.

Which approach takes advantage of Salesforce's capabilities?


-Integrating the AI application directly into the Salesforce framework.


-Contracting with the AI application's developer to build a standalone solution.---


-Asking Salesforce to develop a custom AI application as part of its standard offering.


-Running the AI application on a separate server and uploading the results to Salesforce daily.

Integrating the AI application directly into the Salesforce framework is the approach that takes advantage of Salesforce's capabilities. Salesforce offers various tools and features to support AI integration and customization within its CRM platform, allowing the client to leverage Salesforce's existing infrastructure and data for enhanced predictive analysis of customer behavior.

A large public utilities company approaches Accenture about an enterprise platform. The company's business functions are currently split across multiple older legacy systems, and the company wishes to improve the efficiency of its overall operations.

How can an Accenture Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) help the client?


-by transferring the client's legacy systems to an Accenture cloud-based server


-by migrating and integrating the client's existing data into an enterprise platform


-by assigning separate teams of software engineers to re-program the client's legacy systems


-by coordinating direct negotiations between the client and external platform providers

An Accenture Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) can help the client by migrating and integrating their existing data into an enterprise platform. IPS specializes in consolidating and modernizing legacy systems, enabling the client to streamline their business functions and improve operational efficiency. This involves transferring data from older systems and integrating it into a new, unified enterprise platform to provide a more cohesive and efficient solution for the client's business operations.

Which process of the enterprise platform interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client?


-Core Processing


-Front Office


-Data


-Experience

The process of the enterprise platform that interacts with vendors and manages the entire supply chain for a retail client is the Core Processing. Core Processing involves handling various back-end operations and functions, including vendor management, inventory management, procurement, and logistics. It ensures smooth coordination and management of the supply chain from procurement to delivery, ensuring that products are available to customers in a timely manner.

What distinguishes a SaaS platform from regular software applications?


-Platforms can be deployed very quickly after purchase.


-Platforms require manually-installed software updates.


-Platforms can only be used by a limited number of users.


-Platforms can be extensively customized by the users.

What distinguishes a Software-as-a-Service (SaaS) platform from regular software applications is that SaaS platforms can be extensively customized by the users. Unlike regular software applications that are typically installed on the user's device and have limited customization options, SaaS platforms are hosted on the cloud and accessed online. They allow users to customize various aspects of the platform, such as workflows, settings, and integrations, to align with their specific business needs and processes. This flexibility is one of the main advantages of SaaS platforms.

A Human Resources Manager is looking for a solution that will streamline the tasks involved in hiring, payroll, and employee requests for time off.

Which SaaS platform would benefit the Manager in this situation?


---Workday


-ServiceNow


-Zendesk


-Salesforce

Workday would benefit the Human Resources Manager in this situation. Workday is a popular SaaS platform that specializes in Human Capital Management (HCM) and offers a comprehensive set of features for managing HR tasks such as hiring, payroll, and employee leave requests. It provides tools for recruitment, applicant tracking, payroll processing, time tracking, and absence management, making it an ideal solution for streamlining HR processes in organizations.

A Human Resources Manager is looking for a solution that will streamline the tasks involved in hiring, payroll, and employee requests for time off.

Which SaaS platform would benefit the Manager in this situation?


--Workday


-ServiceNow


-Zendesk


-Salesforce

Workday would benefit the Human Resources Manager in this situation. Workday is a popular SaaS platform that specializes in Human Capital Management (HCM) and offers a comprehensive set of features for managing HR tasks such as hiring, payroll, and employee leave requests. It provides tools for recruitment, applicant tracking, payroll processing, time tracking, and absence management, making it an ideal solution for streamlining HR processes in organizations.

An online retailer is looking to implement an enterprise platform. Which component of the enterprise platform will help the company capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services?


-Data and Insights


-Experience


-Values and Principles


-Core Process

The component of the enterprise platform that will help the online retailer capture, curate, and consume customer information to improve their services is the "Data and Insights" component. This component focuses on collecting and analyzing customer data to gain insights into customer behavior, preferences, and needs. It involves data capture, data storage, data cleansing, data analysis, and data visualization to provide actionable insights that can inform decision-making and improve the retailer's services.

Which describes the "Network Effect?"


-A platform can lose effectiveness if too many users are logged in simultaneously.


-A platform becomes more useful as more people join and use it.


-A platform becomes more useful as its total computing power increases.


-A platform becomes more useful as it is linked to more applications.

The "Network Effect" is described as a platform becoming more useful as more people join and use it. The value and utility of a platform increase as the number of users utilizing the platform grows. With more users, there is a larger network to connect, interact, and exchange information with, leading to increased network effects such as improved communication, collaboration, and knowledge-sharing. This effect often leads to exponential growth and enhances the overall user experience and benefits of the platform.

Which describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud?


-Enterprise platforms are an alternative to hosting solutions on the cloud.


-Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud.


-Data on the cloud can be analyzed and monitored without the need for a platform.


-All enterprise platforms are cloud-based.

The statement that describes the relationship between enterprise platforms and the cloud is: "Enterprise platforms are primarily built around and hosted on the cloud." Enterprise platforms are often designed and developed to leverage cloud computing capabilities, such as scalability, flexibility, and access to shared resources. They utilize cloud infrastructure to host the platform, store data, provide computing power, and offer services to users over the internet. While not all enterprise platforms are exclusively cloud-based, the majority are developed with cloud technology in mind to maximize the benefits it offers.

Which describes a platform?


-a piece of technology that connects people and other technologies together  


-a framework that provides foundational working system for software development


-a ready-to-use software package that performs a specific business function


-an organized collection of data stored electronically on a local computer system or cloud

A platform can be described as a piece of technology that connects people and other technologies together. It acts as a foundation or infrastructure that enables the integration and interaction of different components, users, and systems. Platforms can be software-based, hardware-based, or a combination of both, and they facilitate communication, collaboration, and the development of applications and services. Platforms provide a common ground for various technologies to work together effectively and efficiently.

What is the core function of an enterprise platform?


-It manages the primary functions of a business.


-It connects the supplier with the consumer.


-It helps employees interact with their supervisors.


-It hosts all businesses and other platforms.

The core function of an enterprise platform is to manage the primary functions of a business. An enterprise platform serves as a central hub that supports and integrates various business processes, operations, and activities. It provides tools and functionalities to manage key areas such as sales, customer relationship management, supply chain management, human resources, finance, and more. The primary purpose of an enterprise platform is to streamline and optimize business operations, enhance efficiency, and facilitate collaboration and communication across different departments and functions within an organization.

How does an organization comply with data-usage clauses within data protection regulations such as GDPR or the Data Protection Act?


-Once data is collected it must be used regardless of the purpose.


-Once data is collected it must be used fairly, stored safely, and cannot be kept for longer than necessary.


-Once data is collected it must be used immediately for the registered purpose with the Data Authorities.


-Once data is collected all data must be shared immediately with the Data Authorities.

To comply with data-usage clauses within data protection regulations such as GDPR or the Data Protection Act, an organization must ensure that once data is collected, it is used fairly, stored safely, and not kept for longer than necessary. This means that organizations should have a lawful basis for processing personal data, inform individuals about the purpose and legal basis for processing, and ensure the data is securely stored and protected against unauthorized access. Additionally, organizations should adhere to data retention principles and only retain personal data for as long as necessary for the stated purposes. They are not required to share all collected data immediately with the Data Authorities unless specifically required by a legal obligation under specific circumstances.

Which two companies are among the "Big Six" enterprise platform partners used by Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) business?


-Google and Instagram


-IBM and Cisco


-Oracle and Workday


-Facebook and Twitter
IBM and Oracle are among the "Big Six" enterprise platform partners used by Accenture's Intelligent Platform Services (IPS) business.

A company is considering several Software as a Service (SaaS) platform providers to manage their talent lifecycle of hiring, onboarding, ongoing career development, and payroll.

Where should the company primarily focus when evaluating different SaaS providers?


on the geographic location of the data centers


on the provider’s underlying infrastructure


on the capacity of the provider’s data storage


on the features of the software itself

When evaluating different Software as a Service (SaaS) platform providers to manage the talent lifecycle, the company should primarily focus on the features of the software itself. The software's functionalities, capabilities, and how well they align with the specific needs of the company's talent lifecycle management are crucial factors to consider. This includes evaluating the software's hiring and onboarding capabilities, career development tools, payroll management features, reporting and analytics capabilities, user experience, and integration options. While the geographic location of data centers, provider's infrastructure, and data storage capacity are important considerations, the primary focus should be on the software's features and suitability for the company's talent lifecycle management requirements.

A start-up is expanding overseas and spends an excessive amount of time on recruiting and hiring activities, hindering its ability to focus on the core aspects of its business. How can a Human Capital Management (HCM) platform provider benefit this company?


-by providing a customizable Human Resources system that can be tailored to the company’s needs


-by outsourcing the company’s entire Human Resources function to industry experts


-by offering a standard Human Resources application hosted on the company’s servers


-by building a fully unique Human Resources system from the ground up

A Human Capital Management (HCM) platform provider can benefit the start-up by providing a customizable Human Resources system that can be tailored to the company's needs. This can help streamline and automate the recruiting and hiring activities, saving time and resources for the start-up. The HCM platform can offer features like applicant tracking, onboarding processes, and automated workflows that simplify the recruitment and hiring process. Additionally, the platform can provide tools for managing employee data, performance, training, and payroll, which further frees up time for the start-up to focus on its core business aspects. The customization capability of the HCM platform allows the start-up to align the system with its unique requirements and scale as it expands overseas.
